records.ety: [L. declamatio, from declamare: cf. F. dclamation. See Declaim.]

defs: The act or art of declaiming; rhetorical delivery; haranguing; loud speaking in public; especially, the public recitation of speeches as an exercise in schools and colleges; as, the practice declamation by students.
quotes: The public listened with little emotion, but with much civility, to five acts of monotonous declamation.

defs: A set or harangue; declamatory discourse.
Pretentious rhetorical display, with more sound than sense; as, mere declamation.
